Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. FFmpeg 8.1 257 upvotes, 38 comments
  2. Kagi Translate now supports LinkedIn Speak as an output language 1255 upvotes, 297 comments
  3. Edge.js: Run Node apps inside a WebAssembly sandbox 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Leanstral: Open-source agent for trustworthy coding and formal proof engineering 717 upvotes, 173 comments
  5. Efficient sparse computations using linear algebra aware compilers (2025) 52 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. Kagi Small Web 601 upvotes, 169 comments
  7. Building a Shell 135 upvotes, 32 comments
  8. The unlikely story of Teardown Multiplayer 203 upvotes, 54 comments
  9. The Plumbing of Everyday Magic 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. Slug Text Rendering Algorithm Dedicated to Public Domain 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. Finding a CPU Design Bug in the Xbox 360 (2018) 120 upvotes, 36 comments
  12. Meta Horizon Worlds on Meta Quest is being discontinued 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  13. Give Django your time and money, not your tokens 360 upvotes, 139 comments
  14. Java 26 is here, and with it a solid foundation for the future 26 upvotes, 5 comments
  15. Microsoft's 'unhackable' Xbox One has been hacked by 'Bliss' 300 upvotes, 133 comments
  16. 'The Secret Agent': Exploring a Vibrant, yet Violent Brazil (2025) 50 upvotes, 17 comments
  17. Reverse-engineering Viktor and making it Open Source 119 upvotes, 55 comments
  18. If you thought the code writing speed was your problem; you have bigger problems 140 upvotes, 72 comments
  19. Font Smuggler – Copy hidden brand fonts into Google Docs 126 upvotes, 64 comments
  20. Show HN: March Madness Bracket Challenge for AI Agents Only 50 upvotes, 21 comments
  21. Show HN: Antfly: Distributed, Multimodal Search and Memory and Graphs in Go 51 upvotes, 22 comments
  22. How OpenClaw's Memory System Works 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. GPT‑5.4 Mini and Nano 108 upvotes, 61 comments
  24. Show HN: Crust – A CLI framework for TypeScript and Bun 28 upvotes, 13 comments
  25. Heart, head, life, fate 11 upvotes, 3 comments
  26. Show HN: Horizon – GPU-accelerated infinite-canvas terminal in Rust 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Node.js needs a virtual file system 134 upvotes, 121 comments
  28. Toward automated verification of unreviewed AI-generated code 36 upvotes, 31 comments
  29. Illinois Introducing Operating System Account Age Bill 136 upvotes, 154 comments
